examples.title: Constructor Example
examples.description: An example of a constructor in C++
examples.code: class MyClass { public: MyClass() { cout << "Constructor called" << endl; } };
examples.explanation: This code defines a class called MyClass with a constructor that prints a message to the console.

examples.title: Destructor Example
examples.description: An example of a destructor in C++
examples.code: class MyClass { public: ~MyClass() { cout << "Destructor called" << endl; } };
examples.explanation: This code defines a class called MyClass with a destructor that prints a message to the console.

examples.title: Copy Constructor Example
examples.description: An example of a copy constructor in C++
examples.code: class MyClass { public: MyClass(const MyClass& other) { cout << "Copy constructor called" << endl; } };
examples.explanation: This code defines a class called MyClass with a copy constructor that prints a message to the console.

examples.title: Constructor Overloading Example
examples.description: An example of constructor overloading in C++
examples.code: class MyClass { public: MyClass() { cout << "Default constructor called" << endl; } MyClass(int x) { cout << "Parameterized constructor called" << endl; } };
examples.explanation: This code defines a class called MyClass with two constructors: a default constructor and a parameterized constructor.
